summary: CSRF Protection feature fully tested. All 14 backend tests pass (100%). Frontend integration verified - login returns csrf_token, token stored in localStorage, axios interceptor automatically sends X-CSRF-Token header on all protected requests.
passed_tests: ✅ POST request WITHOUT X-CSRF-Token header returns 403 with 'CSRF token missing'
✅ POST request WITH invalid X-CSRF-Token returns 403 with 'Invalid CSRF token'
✅ POST request WITH valid X-CSRF-Token succeeds (200)
✅ GET requests work without CSRF token (safe methods)
✅ Public login endpoint works without CSRF token
✅ Public register endpoint works without CSRF token
✅ PATCH request requires CSRF token (403 without)
✅ Public verify endpoint works without CSRF
✅ CSRF token unique per login session
✅ Frontend: Login stores csrf_token in localStorage
✅ Frontend: Axios interceptor adds X-CSRF-Token header to all requests
✅ Frontend: Profile update request includes CSRF header
✅ Frontend: Profile update succeeds with valid CSRF token
context_for_next_testing_agent: CSRF protection implemented and verified. Backend: CSRFMiddleware class (server.py lines 6467-6545) validates X-CSRF-Token header on POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH requests. Login endpoint generates csrf_token and returns it in response. Frontend: AuthContext.js stores csrf_token in localStorage after login. Axios interceptor (lines 23-38) automatically adds X-CSRF-Token header to all requests. Exempt paths: /api/auth/login, /api/auth/register, /api/auth/refresh, /api/auth/forgot-password, /api/auth/reset-password, /api/auth/verify-email, /api/verify, /api/public, /api/templates/shared, /api/institutional/login, /api/admin/login, /health, /docs, /openapi.json
